title: Layer registry adoption — declare config.layers[] with L1..L5 substrate/query/composition/dispatch/surface labels
context: config.layers[] (declared in config.schema.json lines 25-38: id + display_name + description) is currently empty in this repo. FGAP entries use 'layer' field with values L1 / L2 / L3 / L4 (free-text, no validation against an installed registry). features.schema.json declares 'L3 Work layer' for features (per items.properties.layer enum L1..L5). PLAN-001 (.project/layer-plans.json) uses Muni L1-L5 restructure plans framework. Inferred layer set per analysis/gsd-2-foundational-intelligence.md + analysis/gsd-2-derivability.md + my conversational scaffolding: L1 substrate (block CRUD / schemas / closure-table primitives), L2 query (filter / resolve / walk / find-references), L3 composition (lenses / roadmap-plan / execution-context), L4 dispatch (jit-agents / workflows / monitors), L5 surface (commands / tools / ceremonies / display). FGAP-052 records the runtime-vs-declaration gap: even when declared, no code path consumes layers[]. Adopting the registry settles the labels canonically; consumption is downstream FGAP-052 work.
decision: Adopt L1..L5 substrate/query/composition/dispatch/surface as THIS repo's canonical layer registry — declared in config.layers[]. Already in widespread use as free-text across FGAPs (layer: L1/L2/L3/L4), features.schema.json (L3 Work layer enum), PLAN-001 layer-plans.json, and analysis docs. Maps directly to pi-context's own package layering: L1 substrate (block CRUD / schemas / closure-table primitives), L2 query (filter / resolve / walk / find-references), L3 composition (lenses / roadmap-plan / execution-context), L4 dispatch (jit-agents / workflows / monitors), L5 surface (commands / tools / ceremonies / display). The 'user-chosen scheme' option (option 2) remains valid for DOWNSTREAM adopters of pi-context — they declare whatever layer scheme fits their project; this enactment is THIS repo's dogfood declaration and does not foreclose other schemes for other adopters. 'Fewer layers (3-tier)' (option 3) rejected: the existing free-text usage already discriminates five layers and conflating any pair loses signal. 'Defer until FGAP-052 consumption lands' (option 4) rejected: declaration without consumption is still useful for validation; consumption is FGAP-052's separate work and not gated by this enactment.
consequences: Every block_kinds[].layer FK references resolve to declared layers[].id.
FGAP layer field validates against the registry (currently free-text).
features.schema.json layer enum aligns with config.layers[].
Layer-aware lens binning + filtering becomes possible once FGAP-052 closes.
Documentation (CLAUDE.md / skill-narratives) references canonical layer labels.

options_considered.label: Adopt L1..L5 substrate/query/composition/dispatch/surface
options_considered.description: Five-tier engineering altitude per pi-context's package layering: L1 substrate (block-api / schemas / closure-table), L2 query (filter-block-items / walk-ancestors / find-references / resolve-items-by-id), L3 composition (lens-view / roadmap-plan / execution-context), L4 dispatch (pi-jit-agents / pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors), L5 surface (commands / pi tools / orchestrator scripts / ceremonies). Aligns with current FGAP layer field usage + analysis docs.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Captures pi-context's actual code architecture; canonical for engineering reasoning; conflates with work-altitude (where features are 'L3 Work layer' which is different conceptually); user-substrate-arc altitude vs engineering-layer ambiguity.

options_considered.label: Adopt different layer scheme appropriate to user's project
options_considered.description: User's project may have different layer altitudes (e.g. for a research repo: L1 raw-data / L2 analyses / L3 papers / L4 publications / L5 dissemination). config.layers[] declares whatever the user's project conceives; built-in defaults are advisory.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Aligns with DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutral; user must choose; pi-context ships no canonical default; documentation guides user authoring.

options_considered.label: Adopt fewer layers (3-tier)
options_considered.description: Simpler scheme: substrate / orchestration / surface. Maps current pi-context less cleanly but reduces conceptual surface.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Easier to reason about; loses granularity that FGAP entries already use.

options_considered.label: Defer layer registry adoption until consumption (FGAP-052) lands
options_considered.description: Don't declare layers[] now; revisit when runtime-honoring lands so declared-vs-consumed don't drift.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Avoids declared-but-symbolic state; blocks any layer-aware lens authoring; conflicts with mandate-007 (no deferring).

references.label: Analysis grounding for L1-L5 inferred labels
DEC-0032 enactment (L1..L5 for THIS repo; downstream-adopter-scheme path preserved as option 2)
